page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.metal-24xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 96 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. With a 1:1 ratio of vCPUs to physical cores, each core runs a single thread, eliminating hyper-threading overhead. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring reliance on external network-attached storage options. It features a high-capacity network interface with a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance.

The arm64-based Graviton architecture offers high resource density and power efficiency, making it a cost-effective option for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores. The balanced memory-to-core ratio and high network throughput make this bare-metal instance suitable for containerized microservices, large-scale web serving, application servers, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server utilizing the arm64 architecture with 96 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Graviton processors. Operating at 2.8 GHz with one thread per core, the instance provides 384.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The server does not feature local storage or integrated GPUs, but it delivers a high baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. The dedicated physical core allocation and high-speed networking make this bare-metal instance highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads. It is well-suited for containerized microservices, enterprise application hosting, and large-scale web serving.
